name: ios_test
description: Start an iOS build + test run on the CodeBuild macOS fleet for a given branch and scheme. Returns immediately with a build_id and status IN_PROGRESS. Then poll ios_build_status with the build_id until the status is no longer IN_PROGRESS. Expected wall time: ~10-15 min for a warm/incremental build (the default), longer for clean_build. Poll about every 60s; each poll is cheap and returns current_phase + phases[] so you can watch progress. Do NOT block on one long foreground sleep — many shells/harnesses kill a command at a ~120s timeout (exit 143), so the wait never actually elapses; instead sleep in short intervals (<=120s) between polls, or run the wait in the background.

branch.description: Git branch or commit SHA to test

scheme.description: Xcode scheme to build and test

device.description: Simulator device name (e.g. 'iPhone 17'). Ignored on the macOS platform.

platform.description: Test platform. 'auto' (default) runs on the iOS Simulator when the scheme exposes one, else native macOS — so SwiftUI/AppKit Mac-app schemes (no simulator destination) just work. Force 'ios' or 'macos' to override. On macOS there is no simulator: 'device'/'record_session' are ignored and the app runs as 'platform=macOS'.

repo.description: GitHub repo URL to build, e.g. https://github.com/owner/name.git (multi-app: point the shared project at this repo for this run). Optional, but the default is a FIXED deploy-time repo that does NOT adapt to your project — if you are testing any repo other than that one, you must pass this, or the checkout fails on a branch that doesn't exist there. The resolved repo is echoed in the ios_test response and in any BUILD_ERROR.

project_dir.description: Subdir (relative to repo root) holding the .xcworkspace/.xcodeproj, e.g. 'ios'. Optional; defaults to the deploy-time PROJECT_DIR (the repo root '.' unless configured otherwise) and does NOT adapt to your repo. Pass '.' when the workspace is at the repo root, or the matching subdir otherwise; a wrong value fails the build with "PROJECT_DIR '<x>' not found". The resolved value is echoed in the ios_test response.

clean_build.description: Force a cold build for this run using a throwaway DerivedData (the warm build state on the reserved Mac is left intact for the next run). Builds are incremental by default; set true when an incremental build looks wrong or for a guaranteed-clean validation run.

record_session.description: Record the entire simulator display to session.mp4 for this run (OS-level capture, independent of any screenshots/video the test itself takes). Off by default. The video is returned as a presigned URL in artifacts.session_video_url and included in artifacts.assets_url. Set true to get visual evidence when the test does not capture its own.

compute_size.description: Reserved Mac size for this run. 'medium' (default; Apple M2 24GB/8vCPU, reliably available) or 'large' (Apple M2 32GB/12vCPU, more cores = faster cold compiles, but may be INSUFFICIENT_CAPACITY in some regions). If the large fleet is not enabled, a large request returns an error so you can retry with medium.

cache_save_threshold.description: Skip re-uploading the warm cache to S3 when this run recompiled fewer than this many files. Xcode recompiles a fixed floor every warm run (e.g. the asset catalog regenerates GeneratedAssetSymbols.swift, cascading ~69 files); set this just above that floor (e.g. 100) so warm reruns skip the ~160-200s save while real source changes still save. Optional; the source-content hash gate already skips most no-op saves, this is a per-repo backstop.

name: ios_build_status
description: Check a CodeBuild build by id and return structured test results. While running, returns status IN_PROGRESS with compute_size, current_phase, and a phases[] timeline (use these as the progress signal). Poll about every 60s in short waits rather than one long foreground sleep, which a ~120s command timeout will kill before it elapses. When done, returns SUCCEEDED | FAILED | BUILD_ERROR | TIMED_OUT with: compute_size (medium|large, where it ran), test_summary, per-test failures, a metrics object (compile count, cache_state cold/partial/warm, cache restore + save seconds, per-phase seconds, test timing) for warm/cold and cache diagnosis, and presigned artifact URLs: xcresult_url (full bundle zip), screenshots[] (every image captured by the test plus the final frame), assets_url (one zip of all visual evidence — images + session video — to download and unzip), session_video_url (whole-session mp4, present only if ios_test set record_session), and a CloudWatch logs link.

build_id.description: CodeBuild build id returned by ios_test

name: ios_list_builds
description: List recent builds for the project with status, current_phase, compute_size, and duration, plus running/queued counts. Use this to see pool/queue state across the medium and large fleets (which builds are RUNNING vs QUEUED) when driving several builds at once — CodeBuild has no per-instance view, so this is how to tell what is actually executing.

limit.description: Max builds to return, newest first (default 20, max 50)

compute_size.description: Optional filter to one fleet size.

name: list_schemes
description: List the Xcode schemes available on a branch, plus the default scheme. Reads the scheme manifest the buildspec publishes on each run; if none exists yet for the branch, returns empty.

name: get_test_logs
description: Get detailed output, class name, duration, and failure screenshots for a single failed test in a completed build. Use this after ios_build_status reports failures to understand why a specific test failed.

test_name.description: Name of the failed test (from failures[].test_name)

name: get_build_log
description: Get build output for a build. While the build is IN_PROGRESS, returns a live_tail of the most recent CloudWatch log lines (real-time progress, before any S3 upload). When the build has finished, returns error_tail (the error: lines plus the last ~100 log lines) and a presigned URL to the full build_output.log. Use this to watch progress mid-build, and whenever ios_build_status returns BUILD_ERROR or a test_summary with total 0 (get_test_logs cannot help there, since it keys off named test failures that do not exist).

build_id.description: CodeBuild build id returned by ios_test

lines.description: How many recent log lines to return for the live tail (default 100, max 500)

name: ios_cancel
description: Stop a running build (CodeBuild StopBuild). Use to abort a wrong or runaway build and free the macOS fleet instead of waiting out the 40-minute timeout. No-op with a clear message if the build has already finished.

build_id.description: CodeBuild build id returned by ios_test
